name: hetzner
description: Manage Hetzner Cloud servers, images, and server types — list and inspect infrastructure, create servers, and power them on or off

envVars.name: HCLOUD_TOKEN
envVars.description: Hetzner Cloud API token (per project, created in Console under Security > API tokens); same env var used by hcloud CLI and Terraform

tools.name: List Servers
tools.description: List servers in the Hetzner Cloud project with optional name, label, and status filtering

label_selector.description: Filter by label selector, e.g. env=prod

status.description: Filter by status: initializing, starting, running, stopping, off, deleting, migrating, rebuilding, unknown

historicalSummary.omitted: network interfaces, image and server type details, and provider-specific payload fields

tools.name: Get Server
tools.description: Get a server by ID, including status, server type, image, and public network info

tools.name: Create Server
tools.description: Create a new server (provisions billable infrastructure)

name.description: Server name (unique per project, valid hostname per RFC 1123)

server_type.description: ID or name of the server type, e.g. cx22

image.description: ID or name of the image, e.g. ubuntu-24.04

location.description: ID or name of the location to create the server in, e.g. fsn1, nbg1, hel1

ssh_keys.description: SSH key IDs or names to inject into the server on creation

start_after_create.description: Start the server right after creation

tools.name: Power On Server
tools.description: Power on a server by ID

tools.name: Power Off Server
tools.description: Cut power to a server by ID (hard power off, like pulling the plug; data may be lost if the OS is running)

tools.name: List Images
tools.description: List available images (system images, backups, snapshots) usable for server creation

type.description: Filter by type: system, backup, snapshot, app

name.description: Filter by exact image name, e.g. ubuntu-24.04

architecture.description: Filter by architecture: x86 or arm

historicalSummary.omitted: image protection, deprecation details, and provider-specific payload fields

tools.name: List Server Types
tools.description: List available server types (sizes) with cores, memory, disk, and pricing

name.description: Filter by exact server type name, e.g. cx22

historicalSummary.omitted: per-location pricing tables and provider-specific payload fields

tools.name: Shutdown Server
tools.description: Gracefully shut down a server by sending an ACPI shutdown signal to the OS — prefer this over Power Off for running workloads

tools.name: Reboot Server
tools.description: Gracefully reboot a server by sending an ACPI reboot signal to the OS

tools.name: List SSH Keys
tools.description: List SSH keys in the project — use these IDs or names in the ssh_keys field of Create Server

name.description: Filter by exact SSH key name

label_selector.description: Filter by label selector, e.g. env=prod

historicalSummary.omitted: public key material and provider-specific payload fields

prompts.title: Infrastructure overview
prompts.prompt: List my Hetzner Cloud servers with their status, server type, and labels.

prompts.title: Spin up a server
prompts.prompt: Create a small Ubuntu server on Hetzner Cloud after confirming the server type, location, and cost with me.

prompts.title: Power cycle a server
prompts.prompt: Power off the Hetzner Cloud server I name, wait for it to stop, then power it back on.

SETUP_GUIDE.title: Hetzner Cloud setup

steps.title: Get a Hetzner Cloud account
steps.description: Sign up at https://www.hetzner.com/cloud and create a project in the Cloud Console (https://console.hetzner.cloud).

steps.title: Generate an API token
steps.description: In the Cloud Console open your project, go to Security > API tokens, and generate a token with Read & Write permissions (Read-only suffices if you skip the write tools).

steps.title: Set the environment variable
steps.description: Copy the token into your .env as HCLOUD_TOKEN — it is shown only once at creation time.

steps.title: Verify access
steps.description: Run List Servers and List Server Types to confirm the token works.

notes: API tokens are scoped to a single Hetzner Cloud project
Rate limit applies per token (check RateLimit-* response headers)
Power Off is a hard power cut — prefer a graceful OS shutdown for running workloads
Creating servers provisions billable infrastructure — confirm server type and location before running write tools
